summary: Manages the encrypted persistence, state, and UI for all application settings. Other features register their settings via settings.ADD and can read/write values via the published events and the settings.UPDATE command.

actions.summary: Registers a new setting definition with the feature. Should be dispatched during startup (e.g., on SYSTEM_STARTING) so that definitions are available before settings are loaded from disk. Idempotent — duplicate keys are ignored.

key.description: A unique, dot-namespaced key for the setting (e.g., 'session.hide_hidden_in_viewer').

type.description: The data type of the setting. Determines the input control rendered in the SettingsView.

label.description: The human-readable label displayed next to the input control.

description.description: A longer description displayed as helper text below the label.

section.description: The section header under which this setting is grouped in the SettingsView.

defaultValue.description: The string-encoded default value used when no persisted value exists. For BOOLEAN, use 'true' or 'false'.

actions.summary: [INTERNAL/UI] Signals that the user has typed into a text-based setting's input field. Updates the transient inputValues for immediate UI responsiveness and starts a 750ms debounce timer before triggering the actual settings.UPDATE persistence. Used only for STRING, NUMERIC_LONG, and STRING_SET types. BOOLEAN settings bypass this and dispatch settings.UPDATE directly.

Commits a new value for a setting, persists all settings to disk (encrypted), and broadcasts settings.publish.VALUE_CHANGED. This is a PUBLIC command — any feature or UI component can dispatch it directly to change a setting value. The SettingsView dispatches it directly for BOOLEAN settings (no debounce) and after a 750ms debounce for text-based settings. Other features dispatch it to programmatically change settings they own (e.g., session.hide_hidden_in_viewer).

value.description: The new string-encoded value. For BOOLEAN, use 'true' or 'false'.

actions.summary: Requests that the application's settings folder be opened in the native file explorer.
Action dispatched by the feature to itself to trigger loading from disk.

Broadcast after settings have been successfully loaded from disk. The payload is a flat JSON object where each key is a setting key and each value is the string-encoded setting value. Features should listen for this to hydrate their transient state from persisted settings (e.g., session hide-hidden flags).

payload_schema.description: A map of setting keys to their string-encoded values. Only keys that were found on disk are included; features should fall back to their registered defaults for missing keys.

actions.summary: Broadcast whenever a setting's value is committed via settings.UPDATE. Features should listen for this to reactively update their transient state when a setting they care about changes at runtime.
